terms.definition: Undamped natural frequency [rad/s]. ωₙ = √(k/m). Depends only on mass and stiffness.
Damping ratio = c/c_c. ζ<1: underdamped; ζ=1: critically damped; ζ>1: overdamped. Typical structures: 0.01-0.05.
Frequency Response Function H(ω)=X(ω)/F(ω). Complex. Magnitude peaks at natural frequencies; phase shifts 180° through resonance.
Eigenvector {φ} of [K-ω²M]{φ}=0. Deformation pattern at natural frequency. Orthogonal w.r.t. [M] and [K].
Experimental Modal Analysis. Extracts f_n, ζ, and mode shapes from measured FRFs. Used to validate FEA models.
Power Spectral Density [m²/Hz or g²/Hz]. Mean-square response per unit frequency. Area = variance = σ².
δ = ln(x₁/x₂). Damping estimate from free decay. ζ ≈ δ/(2π) for light damping.
TR = |output/input|. TR < 1 at r > √2: isolation region. Crossover at r = √2 for all ζ.
Modal Assurance Criterion. Compares two mode shapes: 0=orthogonal, 1=identical. MAC > 0.9: good FEA-test correlation.
σ² ≈ (π/2)·S₀·ωₙ·Q. RMS SDOF response under white noise PSD S₀. Q = 1/(2ζ) = quality factor.
Shaft speed where 1× running frequency = bending natural frequency. Causes large lateral deflection (whirl).
ω² = Σk_i·x_i² / Σm_i·x_i². Upper bound on fundamental frequency. Accurate when assumed shape is close to true mode.
